* fix: START button works on first tap (was requiring double-tap)

level_select_system sets confirmed=true on tap, but game_state_system
checked confirmed AND touch_up on the same frame. Since they run in
order within the same frame, confirmed was set AFTER game_state
already checked it, requiring a second tap.

Fix: game_state_system now checks confirmed without requiring
touch_up — the flag itself is the trigger.

Also: reduced hit padding to 10px (double-tap was the real issue).

Features/payment service (#92)

* feat(payment): implement payment service infrastructure and repositories

- Added PaymentDbContext model snapshot for EF Core migrations.
- Created PaymentInfrastructureExtensions for setting up the payment database context and services.
- Implemented SqlPaymentRepository for payment-related database operations.
- Implemented SqlWalletRepository for wallet-related database operations.
- Added WalletSeeder for seeding initial wallet data.
- Created VNPayGatewayTests for testing VNPay gateway functionality.
- Updated UserService API configuration to include payment scopes and resources.
- Updated appsettings.json to include payment access scope.
- Removed unnecessary appsettings.Development.json from template.

* feat(seed): implement execution strategy for seeding data in various seeders

* fix(review): address CodeRabbit PR #92 review comments

- fix: register EnumerationJsonConverterFactory on AddControllers.AddJsonOptions (controllers were previously skipped)
- fix: introduce IPaymentEventPublisher abstraction so Application layer no longer references Infrastructure.Messaging directly
- fix: GetPaymentQueryHandler and GetWalletQueryHandler now inject IUserContext for ownership enforcement
- fix: PaymentSucceededDomainEventHandler throws on invalid/empty IdempotencyKey instead of publishing Guid.Empty
- fix: PaymentEntityConfiguration.OrderId index made unique; add migration AddUniqueIndexOnPaymentOrderId
- fix: CheckoutSagaStateMachine fails fast on missing Payment:ReturnUrl / Payment:IpnUrl config
- fix: Wallet.Credit/Debit validate amount > 0; BankAccount.Create validates all fields
- fix: split PaymentDomainEvents.cs (6 types) and WalletDomainEvents.cs (3 types) into one-type-per-file
- fix: split EnumerationJsonConverter.cs into EnumerationJsonConverter.cs + EnumerationJsonConverterFactory.cs
- fix: ProcessPaymentWebhookValidator rules now carry .WithState() structured error codes
- fix: replace Swagger UI with Scalar for PaymentService API documentation
- fix: VNPayGateway no longer logs hashData/secureHash/paymentUrl at Info level
- fix: WalletEndpoints pagination bounds validation (page >= 1, 1 <= pageSize <= 100)
- fix: RabbitMqExtensions QueryDelay reduced from 5s to 1s (consistent with AzureServiceBusExtensions)
- fix: ClearCartConsumerDefinition and CreateOrderConsumerDefinition use exponential backoff instead of r.None()
- fix: WalletSeeder checks specific Alice/Bob user IDs instead of AnyAsync; uses execution strategy with ChangeTracker.Clear()
- fix: StoreRefSeeder, CartSeeder, CouponSeeder, ProductRefSeeder re-query existence inside ExecuteAsync delegate
- fix: OrderSeeder loads productRefs/skuRefs inside delegate; replaces Guid.NewGuid() with deterministic BobOrder1PaymentId
- docs: gitnexus-cli SKILL.md recommends env vars over --api-key to avoid shell history leaks

feat: Multi-agent quant framework — Markov regimes, crypto factors, Almgren-Chriss impact, stablecoin hedging, orchestrator (#4)

* feat(research): add Markov-Switching regime detection module

Implements a Bayesian HMM with four market states (LOW_VOL_HIGH_LIQ,
MID_VOL_MID_LIQ, HIGH_VOL_LOW_LIQ, CRISIS) using vol-ratio and Amihud
illiquidity as observable signals.

Key components:
- MarkovRegimeState enum with 4 crypto-calibrated states
- AmihudIlliquidity class (Amihud 2002)
- MarkovRegimeDetector with Bayesian forward-step updates
- Pre-calibrated 4x4 transition matrix for crypto markets
- Gaussian emission model per state
- Confidence-weighted portfolio aggregation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add crypto-native alpha factors module

Implement 10 crypto-specific alpha factors in crypto_factors.py:
- NvtRatioFactor: NVT proxy using price/volume
- OrderImbalanceFactor: Volume-weighted buy/sell pressure
- FundingRateSpreadFactor: Two-tiered funding rate spread
- AmihudIlliquidityFactor: Amihud (2002) illiquidity ratio
- VolatilityOfVolatilityFactor: Vol-of-vol instability signal
- RsiFactory: RSI centered around zero
- BollingerBandWidthFactor: Bollinger Band compression
- VwapDeviationFactor: Price deviation from VWAP
- OBVTrendFactor: On-Balance Volume trend slope
- CTrendAggregateFactor: Elastic Net regularised multi-factor aggregate

commit.message:
fix(api): Rate Limiter — Stale Bucket Eviction and Nested Lock Contention (#4592)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): rate limiter stale bucket eviction and nested lock contention

- Add TTL-based eviction in RateLimiter::allow(): buckets idle > 2×window
  with tokens >= capacity are removed to bound memory growth
- Replace std::mutex with std::shared_mutex in OperationRateLimiter so
  allow()/remaining()/getHeaders() take shared locks, setLimit()/clear()
  take exclusive locks — eliminates nested lock bottleneck under concurrency
- Compute steady_clock::now() before acquiring mutex_ in RateLimiter::allow()
  and remaining(); pass pre-computed time_point into Bucket::refill() and
  Bucket::consume() to keep clock syscalls outside the critical section
- Add <shared_mutex> include
- Update Bucket::refill() and Bucket::consume() signatures to accept
  a time_point parameter
- Add tests: StaleBucketEviction and ConcurrentReadsDontBlock

perf: improve application startup performance

Renderer code splitting:
- Lazy-load 15 components with React.lazy() and Suspense (ChatPane,
  ActivityPanel, SettingsPanel, TerminalPanel, WelcomePane, BottomPanel,
  GitPanel, CommandPalette, CommitComposer, WorkflowPicker, BookmarksPanel,
  SessionSearchPanel, KeyboardShortcutsPanel, DiscoveredToolingModal,
  ProjectSettingsPanel)
- This moves ~1.2 MB of optional dependencies (Lexical, @xyflow/react,
  @xterm/xterm, highlight.js, motion) out of the critical bundle
- Critical bundle reduced from ~1.5 MB to ~759 KB
- Defer JetBrains Mono font load to TerminalPanel (saves ~80 KB at startup)

Main process optimizations:
- Use show: false + ready-to-show on BrowserWindow to eliminate blank flash
- Decouple workspace loading from bootstrap — no longer blocks window, tray,
  and auto-update setup
- Defer sidecar-dependent approval tool pruning to run in background after
  workspace is returned to renderer (removes sidecar spawn from critical path)
- Parallelize independent workspace sync operations (user tooling, project
  tooling, project customization) with Promise.all()

The improvements make file operations and job monitoring more intuitive.

* refactor(permission): rename permission check functions for clarity

- User.CanWrite() → User.CanCreateFilesOrFolders()
- common.CanWrite() → common.CanWriteContentBypassUserPerms()
- common.IsApply() → common.MetaCoversPath()

Improves code readability by making function names more descriptive.
The new MetaCoversPath name clearly indicates it checks if a meta rule
covers a specific path. It better conveys that it's a query function
rather than an action, and the applyToSubFolder parameter is more
explicit than applySub.

Also adds comprehensive test coverage:
- 10 tests for MetaCoversPath core logic
- 6 tests for CanWriteContent
UserPerms
- 7 tests for getReadme
- 5 tests for getHeader
- 6 tests for isEncrypt
- 9 tests for whetherHide

Total: 43 test scenarios covering all path matching and permission
inheritance logic. Tests verify both normal behavior and bug fixes
for Readme/Header information leakage and write permission bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(permission): implement fine-grained user permissions for read/write operations

Add per-user read and write permission controls at the meta level to enable
more granular access control beyond the existing permission flags.

Key changes:
- Add ReadUsers/WriteUsers fields to Meta model with sub-directory inheritance flags
- Implement CanRead and CanWrite permission check functions in server/common
- Filter file list results based on user read permissions
- Add permission checks across all file operations (FTP, HTTP handlers, WebDAV)
- Simplify error handling pattern for MetaNotFound errors throughout codebase

This allows administrators to restrict specific users from accessing or modifying
certain paths, providing finer control over file system permissions.

Note: Batch and recursive operations (FsMove, FsCopy, FsRemove, FsRecursiveMove,
FsBatchRename, FsRegexRename) currently check parent directory permissions only.
Individual item permission checks are not performed for performance reasons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(permission): add comprehensive tests for CanRead, CanWrite, and combined permission checks

Add TestCanRead, TestCanWrite, TestCanAccessWithReadPermissions, and
TestWritePermissionCombinations to validate the three-layer permission
system including nil user/meta, sub-path inheritance, user whitelists,
and root-level restrictions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): use safe type assertion for MetaPassKey to prevent panic

Bearer-token and OPTIONS auth paths do not set MetaPassKey in context,
causing a panic when handlers perform a forced type assertion on nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): treat nil user as system context in CanRead/CanWrite

Previously, CanRead/CanWrite returned false for nil user, causing
filterReadableObjs to return an empty list when fs.List is called from
internal contexts without a user (e.g. context.Background()). A nil user
represents an internal/system call and should bypass per-user restrictions,
consistent with how whetherHide already handles nil user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(fsmanage): prevent path traversal in FsRemove

The previous check only skipped names that resolved to "/", but did not
prevent traversal to sibling directories (e.g. "../secret"), which could
bypass the CanWrite permission check that is only applied to req.Dir.

Replace with a post-join prefix check to ensure each resolved path stays
within reqPath.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): align MetaPassKey behavior with FTP auth logic

For guest users, the WebDAV password input serves as the meta folder
password (consistent with FTP anonymous/guest handling). For authenticated
users, MetaPassKey is set to empty string since their login password is
not the meta folder password.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): require write auth for fs list refresh

* refactor(permission): use MetaCoversPath in CanRead/CanWrite for consistency

Replace inline `(Sub || meta.Path == path)` logic with MetaCoversPath,
consistent with CanWriteContentBypassUserPerms. Also fix a copy-paste
error in the CanWrite comment (read → write).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tests): wait for real worker to be working in concurrency spec to prevent job starvation (#965)

Instead of sleeping 0.5s (hoping the container worker dequeues j1), poll

and correctly models waiting for the real container worker rather than
emulating an extra worker.

* fix: complete package metadata for NuGet, npm, and PyPI publishing

- Add MIT LICENSE file at repo root
- Fix .NET csproj: correct icon path, add Description/Authors/License/URLs/Tags
- Add repository/bugs/homepage/author/keywords to both Node package.json files
- Add authors/keywords/classifiers/project.urls to Python pyproject.toml
- Clean up garbled READMEs for all three packages (remove duplicates, update API examples)
- Add missing README for botas-express package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: use nbgv-python hatch plugin for Python versioning

Replace sed-based version stamping with nbgv-python hatch plugin that
natively produces PEP 440 versions (including pre-release normalization).

- Add nbgv-python>=2.0.0 to build-system requires
- Use dynamic version with hatch nbgv source plugin
- Bump Python to 3.12 in CI, CD, pyproject.toml, and ruff config
- Remove sed version hack from CD workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: publish to GitHub Packages on main, public registries on release

CD now triggers on both main and release/** branches:
- release/** → NuGet.org, npmjs.com, PyPI (production)
- main → GitHub Packages (NuGet, npm), TestPyPI (Python)

npm GitHub Packages requires scoped names, so the publish step
temporarily sets the name to @rido-min/botas.

Additional secrets needed for non-release:
- TEST_PYPI_API_TOKEN (for TestPyPI)
- GITHUB_TOKEN is automatic (for NuGet/npm GitHub Packages)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use npmjs.com with --tag dev instead of GitHub Packages for npm

GitHub Packages npm requires scoped package names which would change
the package identity. Use npmjs.com with --tag dev for non-release
branches instead, keeping the same package name.

* rename: npm package botas → botas-core

Package name 'botas' is taken on npmjs.com. Rename to 'botas-core'
across all package.json files, imports, and documentation.

nbgv-python hatch plugin requires the nbgv CLI at install time,
